### Post by crazyfuturamanoob on 2008-07-23
What drivers should I download for my NVIDIA GeForce 8600 GS, because most drawing things laag like hell, 
don't work or they crash the computer? So this computer is a laptop, Acer Aspire 7520g. Here's my xorg.conf file:
```
# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"fi"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"nVidia Corporation G80 [GeForce 8600M GS]"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:2:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-72
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"nVidia Corporation G80 [GeForce 8600M GS]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	16
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1440x1440"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"

# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
```

Edit: Now my ubuntu is messed up. Read my latest post.

This is a serious problem I have encountered with 3 computers already. Thanks for all replies.

### Post by PmDematagoda on 2008-07-23
Did you try installing the proprietary Nvidia drivers through System>Administration>Hardware Drivers? From your xorg.conf file you are using the nv driver, which could be a reason for all the graphics problems.

---

### Post by crazyfuturamanoob on 2008-07-23
> **PmDematagoda said:**
> Did you try installing the proprietary Nvidia drivers through System>Administration>Hardware Drivers? From your xorg.conf file you are using the nv driver, which could be a reason for all the graphics problems.
But the "hardware drivers" window is completely empty, and there is only "close" button, and "help" button. And with apt/synaptic it can't find the nvidia-glx drivers packages at all! HELP!!

### Post by tuxxy on 2008-07-23
You could try and isntall them using Envy in the repository then,

```
sudo apt-get install envyng-gtk
```


good luck

---

### Post by PmDematagoda on 2008-07-23
Perhaps you may not have the proper repositories active, did you try going to Software Sources in System>Administration>Software Sources and seeing if all the repositories in the Ubuntu Software tab are selected?

---

### Post by crazyfuturamanoob on 2008-07-24
Ok, I used EnvyNG to install the drivers. But now this computer is totally messed up:
Resolution is 800x600 and I can't change it!! At every bootup it asks resolution and some driver, but no matter what i type in, the settings don't take effect!
Then it goes to login screen, with resolution 800x600. I login. Now I can see the restricted drivers in System>Administration>Hardware Drivers, but the box is empty. If I fill in the box, and put the driver in use, it says i gotta reboot. If I reboot, the driver will not be in use, and the box is empty again!! and at System>Administration>nvidia x server settings it says: > You do not appear to be using the NVIDIA X driver. Please edit your X configuration file (just run `nvidia-xconfig` as root), and restart the X server.
running "nvidia-xconfig" doesnt do anything, and i dont know how to restart x-server what to do now?? Reinstall ubuntu? Remove ubuntu and install another os? and the drivers aren't anyway working HELP!!!
